description_long: This office building features a rectangular, elongated main structure with three visible floors, characterized by a flat roof and a uniform grid of windows along its facade. The exterior is clad in light beige or tan concrete panels, with a secondary, attached block that appears to serve as a service or loading area, distinguished by its darker, industrial finish. The main entrance is recessed and accessible via a small staircase, while additional doors and loading bays are visible on the side structure. The windows are arranged in a regular, horizontal banding pattern, each floor separated by subtle horizontal lines. The building lacks any visible signage or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian, functional design. The overall appearance suggests a mid-to-late 20th-century Modernist or utilitarian architectural style, suitable for administrative or commercial office use.
description: A three-story, rectangular office building with a flat roof, light concrete exterior, and a secondary attached service block. The facade features a regular grid of windows and a recessed main entrance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the attached service block with its darker, industrial finish and visible loading bay, as well as the side of the main office building.
description_view_1: The front facade is visible, showing the main entrance, three rows of regularly spaced windows, and the full length of the building.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the long side of the building, the regular window pattern, and the connection between the main structure and the service block.
description_view_3: The rear and side of the building are visible, showing additional doors and the back of the service block, with minimal architectural detailing.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular horizontal bands of rectangular windows, evenly spaced across each floor, with consistent repetition and alignment.
